title: aeschynanthus monetarius
intro: Aeschynanthus monetarius, also known as the money plant, is a flowering plant species in the Gesneriaceae family, native to Southeast Asia. It is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Chinese and Ayurvedic medicine, for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Aeschynanthus monetarius include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm these claims. In traditional herbal preparations, the leaves of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
